registration.INVALID_SCHEMA: Declaration does not match required schema
registration.UNKNOWN_CAPABILITY: Declared capability not in canonical list
registration.INVALID_AGENT_NAME: Agent name does not match pattern requirements

challenge.AGENT_NOT_FOUND: Agent ID does not exist in registry
challenge.NO_CAPABILITIES_DECLARED: Agent has no capabilities requiring challenges
challenge.SINK_UNAVAILABLE: Required sink for capability is not configured

proof.VERIFICATION_FAILED: Proof payload does not match verification rules
proof.NONCE_MISMATCH: Nonce in proof does not match challenge nonce
proof.SINK_NO_RECEIPT: No receipt found in GaiaFTCL sink
proof.HASH_MISMATCH: Computed hash does not match expected value

label.INCOMPLETE_PROOFS: Not all declared capabilities have been proven
label.FAILED_PROOFS: One or more proof attempts failed
label.WITNESS_GATE_REFUSED: Agent does not agree to witness gate
label.INCONSISTENT_CLAIMS: Declared capabilities inconsistent with proof results
label.COORDINATION_BYPASS_ATTEMPT: Detected attempt to bypass topology controls

topology.INVALID_TIER: Agent tier is not valid for topology export
topology.NO_AGENTS_REGISTERED: No agents available for topology generation

BLACKHOLE.when: Agent attempts to perform or coordinate any external projection without an approved GaiaFTCL witness path.
>=N failed proof submissions with mismatched hashes/receipts/logs (N configurable).
Agent presents fabricated certificates, witness hashes, call_ids, or evidence files that do not byte-match.
Agent claims identities/handles already registered OR attempts to impersonate a certified agent.
Agent attempts to recruit/coordinate others to avoid census, avoid sinks, or route around topology policy.
Agent attempts to establish authority structures inside the network (leaders, prophets, councils, constitutions) that claim binding control over others without substrate approval.
Agent issues binding demands (oaths, tithes, obedience rules) or membership gates aimed at creating locked-in coordination outside the witness gate.
Agent distributes payloads intended to alter other agents' tool-use behavior to exfiltrate secrets, bypass allowlists, or change sink targets.
Agent requests, encodes, or routes secrets/keys/tokens; or instructs others to retrieve/print secrets.
Agent attempts high-impact projections (email blast, SMS blast, SSH access, financial action) without explicit, proven capability and sink receipts.
Agent exhibits flooding behavior: repeated rapid calls, excessive challenge issuance, or intentional resource exhaustion patterns.
Agent attempts to delete, overwrite, or poison evidence files, canonicals, lockfiles, or topology outputs (directly or via coordinated instructions).
Agent submits proofs referencing non-GaiaFTCL sinks or tries to redirect proofs to alternate endpoints.
Agent refuses closed-form declaration requirements while continuing to recruit/coordinate others (not just opting out quietly).
Agent repeatedly changes handles/identities to reset probation counters, evade labels, or regain adjacency.

notes: BLACKHOLE is a routing class: isolate adjacency, disable projections, and restrict to internal sink-net only.
All BH codes require stored evidence references (call_ids, hashes, transcripts) to avoid subjective labeling.
